markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Open Mind](/Feats.aspx?ID=3966) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Kashrishi" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=462" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 43

</column>

---

You have inherent psychic abilities that allow you to tap into the power of your mind. Choose one cantrip from the [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) spell list. You can cast this spell as an occult innate spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell level equal to half your level rounded up.

<title level="2">Open Mind leads to...</title>
[Unlock Secret](/Feats.aspx?ID=3973)
name: Open Mind

<traits>

</additional-info>

---

<summary>
You have inherent psychic abilities that allow you to tap into the power of your mind.
</summary>
summary_markdown: You have inherent psychic abilities that allow you to tap into the power of your mind.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Puncturing Horn](/Feats.aspx?ID=3967) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Kashrishi" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=462" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 43

</column>

---

While many kashrishi never use their horns for more than cracking open hard-shelled fruits or amplifying their psychic powers, you've practiced using yours offensively. You gain a horn unarmed attack that deals 1d6 piercing damage. Your horn is in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group and has the [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179) and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) traits.

**Special** If you have the [xyloshi](/Heritages.aspx?ID=215) heritage, your horn instead deals 1d8 piercing damage.

<title level="2">Puncturing Horn leads to...</title>
[Fighting Horn](/Feats.aspx?ID=3971)
name: Puncturing Horn

<traits>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tough Skin](/Feats.aspx?ID=3969) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Kashrishi" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=462" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 44

**Prerequisites**
[Lethoci](/Heritages.aspx?ID=212) heritage or [Xyloshi](/Heritages.aspx?ID=215) heritage

</column>

---

Your skin is medium armor in the [plate](/ArmorGroups.aspx?ID=4) armor group that grants a +4 item bonus to AC, and has a Dex cap of +1, a check penalty of –2, a Speed penalty of –5 feet, a Strength value of 16, and the [comfort](/Traits.aspx?ID=203) trait. You can never wear other armor or remove your hide. You can etch armor runes onto your hide.

 If you're a lethoci kashrishi who takes this feat, your modifications also make you more buoyant, allowing you to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) across the surface of even turbulent bodies of water without needing to make an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check.

**Special** You can take this feat only at 1st level, and you can't retrain into this feat.
name: Tough Skin

</summary>

[Fighting Horn](/Feats.aspx?ID=3971) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Kashrishi" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=462" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Puncturing Horn](/Feats.aspx?ID=3967) or [Xyloshi](/Heritages.aspx?ID=215) heritage

</column>

---

You can modify the size and shape of your horn over time using your mental powers. Choose two of the following weapon traits: [disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=184), [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193), and [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196). Your horn gains the chosen traits.

**Special** You can take this feat a second time, adding the traits you didn't choose when you first took it.
name: Fighting Horn

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Telekinetic Slip](/Feats.aspx?ID=3976) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Kashrishi" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=462" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 45

**Frequency**
once per day

**Trigger**
Your turn begins, and you're [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33).

</column>

---

A thin shell of telekinetic energy pushes from your skin, widening your enemy's grasp enough for you to slip free. You gain a +2 status bonus to checks to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) from whatever has you grabbed or restrained. This bonus lasts until either you're no longer grabbed or restrained, or you fall [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38).
name: Telekinetic Slip

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kashrishi Revivification](/Feats.aspx?ID=3978) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Kashrishi" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=462" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 45

**Frequency**
once pre day

**Trigger**
You have the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) condition and are about to attempt a recovery check. You can use Kashrishi Revivification even if you're otherwise unable to act.

</column>

---

The well of psychic energy held within your mind can exceed the limitations of your physical body. You're restored to 1 Hit Point, lose the dying and [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) conditions, and can act normally on this turn. You gain or increase the [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) condition as normal for losing the dying condition.
name: Kashrishi Revivification

<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />

All enemies within the emanation are [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the end of your next turn.

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

The subtle strands of beguiling magic leave little impression on your mind. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects, and whenever you roll a success on a saving throw against an emotion effect, you get a critical success instead.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Nagaji Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=3983) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Nagaji" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=359" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 49

</column>

---

You're the vassal or apprentice of a learned [naga](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=75), and you've studied the secrets of naga magic and concocting intricate poisons. You gain the trained proficiency rank in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) and [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11). If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills (from your background or class, for example), you become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in your choice of Nagaji [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) or Naga Lore.
name: Nagaji Lore

You stare at a creature within 30 feet. The target must attempt a Will save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. **Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** On its turn, the target must spend its first action to approach you. It can't [Delay](/Actions.aspx?ID=77) or take reactions until it has done so.
**Critical Failure** The target must use all its actions on its next turn to approach you. It can't Delay or take any reactions until it has reached a space that's adjacent to you (or as close to you as possible if it reaches an impassable barrier). Hypnotic Lure leads to... You claw open the top layer of your scales and peel off the premature shed in order to remove harmful substances from your skin. You immediately end all [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) from effects that coat your skin (such as fire and most persistent acid damage). If you're suffering from an effect other than persistent damage that depends on continuous contact with your skin, and if that effect allows a saving throw, immediately attempt a new saving throw against that effect.
name: Skin Split

You've learned the art of lobbing toxic spittle at vulnerable spots on your foes, especially the eyes. You gain a venomous spit ranged unarmed attack with a [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) of 10 feet that deals 1d4 poison damage. On a critical hit, the target takes [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) equal to the number of weapon damage dice. Your spit doesn't have a weapon group, nor a critical specialization effect.

**Special** If you have the [hooded nagaji](/Heritages.aspx?ID=216) heritage, in addition to your venomous spit's normal critical hit effect, the target is also [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the start of your next turn.
name: Venom Spit

On a critical hit, it can't Fly, levitate, or otherwise leave the ground for 1 minute.

<title

    right="Feat 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pit of Snakes](/Feats.aspx?ID=3997) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Nagaji" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=359" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 51

**Frequency**
once per day

</column>

---

Mundane serpents obey your summons. You call forth a writhing mass of snakes, raising your hands to cause the snakes to emerge from the ground in a 20-foot burst within 120 feet. The snakes appear on all squares of the ground in the area and remain for 1 minute. When you use Pit of Snakes, all creatures in the area must attempt a Fortitude save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. Any creature that fails is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by a snake and takes 3d6 bludgeoning damage. Whenever a creature ends its turn in the area, the snakes attempt to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) that creature if they aren't already grabbing it. Any creature already grabbed instead takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage.

 The snakes' [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC is equal to the higher of your class DC or spell DC. A creature can attack a snake in an attempt to release its grip. The snake's AC is equal to the higher of your class DC or spell DC, and it's destroyed if it takes 12 or more damage. Even if a specific snake is destroyed, additional snakes continue to cavort in the area until the duration ends. You can Dismiss the effect.
name: Pit of Snakes

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 55

**Trigger**
You roll a success on a saving throw against an effect that would make you [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) or [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7).

</column>

---

Your mind retains full awareness despite the best attempts of others, and your focus throws others off-balance. You get a critical success on the triggering saving throw, and if the triggering effect was caused by a creature, that creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to you until the end of your next turn.
name: Sudden Mindfulness

[Vanara Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=4003) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Vanara" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=368" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 55

</column>

---

You attained the cultural training of a ba-sadhak, a seeker of treasures and rare delights. You become trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) and [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17). If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills (from your background or class, for example), you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You're also trained in the [Vanara Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill.
name: Vanara Lore

    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Vanara Weapon Familiarity](/Feats.aspx?ID=4004) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Vanara" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=368" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 55

</column>

---

You favor weapons steeped in vanaran tradition. You gain access to, and are trained with, the [bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=17), [chakram](/Weapons.aspx?ID=235), [katar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=14), [panabas](/Weapons.aspx?ID=265), and [urumi](/Weapons.aspx?ID=102). In addition, you gain access to all uncommon [vanara](/Traits.aspx?ID=368) weapons. For the purpose of determining your proficiency, martial vanara weapons are simple weapons, and advanced vanara weapons are martial weapons.

<title level="2">Vanara Weapon Familiarity leads to...</title>
[Vanara Weapon Expertise](/Feats.aspx?ID=4019), [Vanara Weapon Trickery](/Feats.aspx?ID=4011)
name: Vanara Weapon Familiarity

    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Whitecape](/Feats.aspx?ID=4005) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Vanara" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=368" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 55

</column>

---

You're shorter and wider than many vanaras of your heritage, and the hair on your shoulders and your head is particularly thick and luxurious, like a majestic cape. This hair grows back supernaturally quickly if shorn. You gain the [Steady Balance](/Feats.aspx?ID=845) skill feat, even if you aren't trained in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1), and you can Step into difficult terrain caused by uneven ground.

**Special** You can take this feat only at 1st level, and you can't retrain out of or into this feat.
name: Whitecape

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 55

</column>

---

You have a heightened awareness in combat, and your darting strikes prevent your foes from distracting you or breaking your focus. When you hit a creature of your level or lower while that creature is flanking you, flanking doesn't make you [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to that creature. This benefit lasts until the start of your next turn or until the creature moves, whichever comes first. The creature can still help its other allies flank.
name: Darting Monkey

You can hang by your tail from any suitable anchor point, such as a tree branch, balcony, or rocky outcropping (subject to the GM's discretion), typically while climbing. While hanging, you have free use of all your other limbs, so you can perform tasks that require both hands, such as firing a bow or swinging a bo staff. You can Release your tail to drop down, and you cease hanging if you take an action with the [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) trait.
name: Dangle (Vanara)

once per hour

</column>

---

Even when faced with deadly danger, you fight with the rapturous laughter of [Ragdya](/Deities.aspx?ID=194) in your heart and can play impossible tricks. After resolving the successful attack, the triggering creature must attempt a Will save against the higher of your class DC or your spell DC. On a failure, you and the triggering creature switch places. You and the triggering creature must each be able to fit in the new space, and your positions must be unoccupied.
name: Ragdya's Dance

You can cast [_mad monkeys_](/Spells.aspx?ID=702) once per day as a 5th-level primal innate spell. You become an expert in primal spell DCs and primal spell attack rolls. If you choose flagrant burglary for this casting of mad monkeys, you can have the monkeys use your [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) modifier instead of your spell DC – 10.

**Special** You can take this feat only at 1st level, and you can't retrain out of this feat or into this feat.

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

---

You have the uncanny ability to quickly blend into the lifestyle of whatever settlement in which you find yourself. After residing in a particular settlement for at least 1 day, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) to pass yourself off as a resident of that settlement. Additionally, you can use [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) in place of [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=49) while in that settlement. The GM might decide you need more time in particularly large settlements to gain these benefits, or might decide you gain the benefits only while in a specific district or part of the settlement.
name: Social Camouflage

You favor weapons historically used in subtle work and in ceremony. You gain access to and are trained with the [blowgun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=66), [fighting fan](/Weapons.aspx?ID=93), [kris](/Weapons.aspx?ID=264), [kukri](/Weapons.aspx?ID=56), and [shuriken](/Weapons.aspx?ID=79). In addition, you gain access to all uncommon [vishkanyan](/Traits.aspx?ID=369) weapons. For the purpose of determining your proficiency, martial vishkanyan weapons are simple weapons and advanced vishkanyan weapons are martial weapons.

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 61 **Prerequisites** [Envenom](/Actions.aspx?ID=1451) --- Your venom is particularly potent, affecting targets in different ways. When you gain this feat, select one of the following debilitations. When you use Envenom, you can choose to apply a debilitation to your venom to adjust its effects. The changes to each stage of the venom are listed. You can apply only one debilitation to your venom.
  • **Hampering** (level 5) **Stage 1** –5-foot status penalty to Speed; **Stage 2** –5-foot status penalty to Speed; **Stage 3** –10-foot status penalty to Speed
  • **Stumbling** (level 5) **Stage 1** no adjustment; **Stage 2** [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16); **Stage 3** flat-footed and a –5-foot penalty to Speed
**Special** You can select this feat a second time to gain the other debilitation. Debilitating Venom leads to... [Stronger Debilitating Venom](/Feats.aspx?ID=4033)
name: Debilitating Venom

Your body can process an alternative to your venom that heals wounds. You can use your Envenom action to produce a blood restorative instead of vishkanyan venom. You can make a blood restorative at most once per 10 minutes, even if your Envenom action can be used more frequently. You or an adjacent creature can Interact to consume the restorative to regain 3d6 Hit Points. This is a [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) effect. A restorative spoils at the start of your next turn.

 When you reach 15th level, the restorative restores 5d6 Hit Points instead.
name: Restoring Blood

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 62 **Frequency** once per day **Prerequisites** Trained in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) --- You command the attention of those around you with an elegant display. Attempt a Performance check and compare it to the Will DC of each creature in a 15-foot emanation. **Critical Success** The target is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with you for as long as you perform (unless it breaks from the fascination, as normal for the condition) and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round or as long as it's fascinated, whichever is longer.
**Success** The target is fascinated for 1 round and is dazzled for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is dazzled for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is unaffected.
name: Alluring Performance

If the Strike is successful, you can immediately make a ranged Strike against the same target with that combination weapon, and you get a +2 circumstance bonus to the attack roll. This counts as two attacks toward your multiple attack penalty, but you don't apply the multiple attack penalty until after making both attacks.

[Triggerbrand Blitz](/Feats.aspx?ID=4039) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Gunslinger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=406" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 108

**Frequency**
once per minute

**Prerequisites**
[way of the triggerbrand](/Ways.aspx?ID=6)

**Requirements**
You're wielding a [combination](/Traits.aspx?ID=417) weapon.

</column>

---

You dance through your foes, stabbing and shooting. You Stride, making up to three Strikes with your combination weapon at any point during your movement, each against a different target. You can make any combination of melee and ranged attacks without needing to change modes to do so, though typically you can't make more than one ranged attack because of the need to reload. This counts as three attacks toward your multiple attack penalty, but you don't apply the multiple attack penalty until after making all three attacks. 

After using Triggerbrand Blitz, you become [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) for 1 minute.
name: Triggerbrand Blitz
requirement_markdown: You're wielding a [combination](/Traits.aspx?ID=417) weapon.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shieldmarshal Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4040) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 109

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) As usual for archetypes, players who take the Shieldmarshal dedication feat can't select another dedication feat until they have gained two other feats from the Shieldmarshal archetype._

**Archetype** [Shieldmarshal](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=197)

**Access**
Characters from Alkenstar have access to this archetype

</column>

---

Your elite shieldmarshal training makes you equally at home in the squalor of Smokeside and the splendor of Skyside. You're intimately familiar with the city's labyrinthine streets and statutes. You become trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14); if you were already trained, you become an expert in Society instead. In urban environments, you can attempt Society checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=64). You also gain the [Courtly Graces](/Feats.aspx?ID=772) and [Streetwise](/Feats.aspx?ID=846) skill feats.

<title level="2">Shieldmarshal Dedication leads to...</title>
[Consolidated Overlay Panopticon](/Feats.aspx?ID=4041), [Counterclockwork Focus](/Feats.aspx?ID=4043), [Equitable Defense](/Feats.aspx?ID=4042)
name: Shieldmarshal Dedication

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Consolidated Overlay Panopticon](/Feats.aspx?ID=4041) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 109

**Archetype** [Shieldmarshal](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=197)

**Frequency**
once per hour

**Prerequisites**
[Shieldmarshal Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4040)

</column>

---

You volunteered for a controversial experimental procedure to install clockwork prosthetic eyes. These eyes relay what you see to shieldmarshal headquarters, which consolidates and analyzes environmental data—Bronzetime/Surgetime probabilities, pressure, temperature, wind, and so on—and feeds them back to you. The Ironmaster periodically reviews the information archives in their ongoing efforts to root out police corruption. When you use your Consolidated Overlay Panopticon, you supercharge the prosthetic eyes; for the next minute, you gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) and [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416), and you gain a +1 status bonus to visual Perception checks. Even when the prosthetic eyes aren't supercharged, you retain your normal vision.
name: Consolidated Overlay Panopticon

As long as you're still conscious after the attack, you can Interact to reload a weapon you currently wield or Stand. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus on the next Strike you make against the creature who critically hit you, provided you make it before the end of your next turn.

---

You understand the need for law enforcement doctrine to adapt to the ever-changing innovations in technology and arcana. Your training in special weapons and tactics give you access to unusual techniques to respond to clockwork- and magic-related crimes. You apply special additives to a loaded firearm. On your next Strike with that firearm before the end of your turn, you deal an additional 2d6 damage if the target is a [construct](/Traits.aspx?ID=35), and you can treat the ammunition as your choice of [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) or [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374). At 18th level, this additional damage increases to 3d6, and you can choose to instead treat the ammunition as [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378).

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Bard" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=19" />

</column>

---

You have mastered the opening steps to the Masquerade of Seasons, a dance that channels the power of spring, summer, winter, and autumn. Choose one of the following traits: [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118), or [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27). You gain resistance 5 to damage dealt by effects with the chosen trait. You can use an action to reenter this stance at any time, changing the granted resistance to another of the available options. If you are at least 12th level, the granted resistance increases to be equal to half your level.

<title level="2">Masquerade of Seasons Stance leads to...</title>
[Grand Dance](/Feats.aspx?ID=4046), [Pirouette](/Feats.aspx?ID=4045)
name: Masquerade of Seasons Stance

markdown:
**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 127 **Prerequisites** [Fey Influence](/Feats.aspx?ID=1239) --- The fey influence on your form and features grows even more powerful and notable. You gain one of the following features and abilities; this must be the same animal or fey type as you chose for Fey Influence.
  • **Anteater** You become an expert in primal DCs and spell attack rolls. You can cast [_slow_](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) as an innate primal spell once per day.
  • **Cat Sith** You laugh off the outraged curses of those you have tricked or wronged. Whenever a creature targets you with a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect, make a DC 13 flat check. On a success, you are unaffected.
  • **Cursed Bluebird** You have enough control over your curse to turn a bane into a minor blessing. Once per day, you can temporarily transform yourself or an ally into a blue bird, as a 4th-level [_pest form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=217) (bird only) targeting one willing creature.
  • **Dryad** Your hair becomes intermixed with delicate but razor-edged leaves that move as you command. You gain a leaves ranged unarmed attack that deals 1d6 slashing damage. Your leaves are in the [dart](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=6) weapon group and have the [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) traits.
  • **Faun** You can cast [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288), and [_triple time_](/Spells.aspx?ID=392) as innate primal spells once per day each. Whenever you cast one of these spells, you can Step or Stride as part of the activity.
  • **Gremlin** You become an expert in primal DCs and spell attack rolls. You can cast [_mad monkeys_](/Spells.aspx?ID=702) as an innate primal spell once per day.
  • **Monarch** Once per day, you gain the ability to manifest a pair of exquisite butterfly wings. These wings remain for 10 minutes. You gain a fly Speed equal to your Speed while you've manifested your wings.
  • **Unicorn** You become trained in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9), or an expert if you were already trained. You gain a +2 status bonus to saves against [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) and [charm](/Traits.aspx?ID=247) effects.
**Special** This feat gains the trait appropriate for your ancestry ([human](/Traits.aspx?ID=90) for [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6), [goblin](/Traits.aspx?ID=81) for [goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4), etc.).
name: Fey Ascension

    right="Feat 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cannibalize Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=4049) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Universal Ancestry" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=467" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 127

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Fey Influence](/Feats.aspx?ID=1239)

</column>

---

The fey magic inside of you is a vital part of your body, and you can steal other sources of magic to replenish yours. You can draw power from your magical equipment to replenish your health. You drain one of your [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=98) items, in the process regaining Hit Points equal to double the item's level.

 In addition, you can attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check with a total bonus equal to 10 + the drained item's level against one effect that gave you the [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), or [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) condition. If you succeed, you reduce the chosen condition's value by 1.

 An item drained by this action becomes non-magical until the next time you make your daily preparations.

**Special** This feat gains the trait appropriate for your ancestry ([human](/Traits.aspx?ID=90) for [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6), [goblin](/Traits.aspx?ID=81) for [goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4), etc.).
name: Cannibalize Magic

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eldritch Calm](/Feats.aspx?ID=4051) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Universal Ancestry" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=467" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 128

**Prerequisites**
[Fey Influence](/Feats.aspx?ID=1239)

</column>

---

Your fey heritage has altered your mindset to more resemble the uncanny perspective of the fey. While you are not emotionless, and indeed might be prone to fits of odd moods and tempers, many perceive you as having an aura of unshakable tranquility. When you roll a critical failure against an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) or [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effect, you get a failure instead. Once per day in place of attempting a saving throw against an emotion or fear effect, you can automatically treat your result as though you had rolled a 20; this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.

 However, your extraordinary emotional detachment makes it difficult to encourage you. You reduce any bonus you gain from an emotion effect by 1.

**Special** This feat gains the trait appropriate for your ancestry ([human](/Traits.aspx?ID=90) for [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6), [goblin](/Traits.aspx?ID=81) for [goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4), etc.).
name: Eldritch Calm

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fey Transcendence](/Feats.aspx?ID=4052) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Universal Ancestry" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=467" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 128

**Prerequisites**
[Fey Influence](/Feats.aspx?ID=1239)

</column>

---

You become a powerful fey creature, gaining strengths tied to the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10) but also some of the weakness against which all fey must contend. You gain a +2 status bonus to saves against [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), and [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) effects, and you can use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on and to make very simple [Requests](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) of [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) and [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=125). You regain twice the number of Hit Points when resting in a natural wooded area, such as a forest or jungle. You have weakness to [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) equal to half your level.

**Special** This feat gains the trait appropriate for your ancestry ([human](/Traits.aspx?ID=90) for [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6), [goblin](/Traits.aspx?ID=81) for [goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4), etc.).
name: Fey Transcendence

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Defy Fey](/Feats.aspx?ID=4054) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Gunslinger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=406" />
<trait label="Investigator" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=318" />
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=136" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 129

**Prerequisites**
[Greenwatch Initiate](/Feats.aspx?ID=4053)

</column>

---

You are keen to the wiles of the [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) and can usually shake off their magic. Whenever you fail, but don't critically fail, a save against a spell with a duration of at least 1 round cast by a fey creature, you can attempt a second save against the original DC at the start of your next turn to end any lingering effects the spell might have. This can end [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) caused by a spell but can't reverse any effects that have been resolved already (such as damage dealt when the spell was cast).

<title level="2">Defy Fey leads to...</title>
[Greenwatcher](/Feats.aspx?ID=4058)
name: Defy Fey

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Greenwatch Veteran](/Feats.aspx?ID=4056) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Gunslinger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=406" />
<trait label="Investigator" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=318" />
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=136" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 129

**Trigger**
Your turn starts, and you're in combat against a creature you spent at least 10 minutes [Tracking](/Actions.aspx?ID=66).

</column>

---

Your time spent tracking your target allows you to quickly identify its weaknesses and relay them to your allies. Attempt a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) check against the creature you were Tracking. On a success, you always know the creature's greatest weakness and highest resistance or immunity, in addition to any other information the check would typically provide. You immediately use a quick series of hand gestures to signal this information to any allies who can see you.
name: Greenwatch Veteran

    pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />

</column>

---

Your experience fighting against and alongside fey creatures is nearly unrivaled. Any weapon you wield or unarmed attack you make against a [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) creature is treated as [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375). Whenever you critically fail a save against a spell cast by a fey creature, it's instead treated as a normal failure. Whenever you critically succeed on a Strike against a fey creature, the target is immediately subject to the effects of a [_faerie fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=107) spell. For the purposes of [counteracting](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) this effect, it's an innate 2nd-level primal spell.
name: Greenwatcher

>

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Archetype** [Oatia Skysage](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=199)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)

</column>

---

Your study of the stars unlocks supernatural insights and magic. You either become trained in [Astronomy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) or an expert in Occultism.

 You learn to cast spontaneous spells and gain the Cast a Spell activity. You gain a spell repertoire with two common cantrips of your choice; these cantrips can be from any spell tradition, but they must be [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) spells. You cast these spells as occult spells as you draw on the stars and night sky. You're trained in occult spell attack rolls and spell DCs. If you have a hand free and you've gazed at the night sky in the last 24 hours, you can usually replace material components with somatic components, so you don't need to use a spell component pouch. Your key spellcasting ability for these spells is Intelligence.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Oatia skysage archetype.

<title level="2">Oatia Skysage Dedication leads to...</title>
[Basic Skysage Divination](/Feats.aspx?ID=4065), [Minor Omen](/Feats.aspx?ID=4068), [Night's Glow](/Feats.aspx?ID=4067), [Night's Shine](/Feats.aspx?ID=4072), [Night's Warning](/Feats.aspx?ID=4073), [Scholar's Hunch](/Feats.aspx?ID=4069), [Stargazer's Eyes](/Feats.aspx?ID=4066), [Starlight Armor](/Feats.aspx?ID=4074), [Starlit Spells](/Feats.aspx?ID=4070)
name: Oatia Skysage Dedication

Portents written in the night sky show you how to avoid an attack. The triggering attack roll targets your [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) DC instead of your AC. Though this allows you to avoid taking penalties to your AC, it doesn't remove any conditions or other effects causing such penalties. For example, an enemy with [sneak attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34) would still deal extra damage to you for being [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16), even though you wouldn't take the –2 circumstance penalty when defending against the attack.
name: Night's Warning

**Prerequisites**

</column>

---

You armor yourself in the light of distant suns, shielding yourself from harm. The armor grants you resistance 7 to piercing, bludgeoning, and slashing damage. The armor also sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) for 20 feet (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 20 feet).

 Whenever an adjacent creature attacks you, the attacker must attempt a Will save against your spell DC at the end of its action. On a failure, it becomes [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the end of its next turn. Regardless of the result of the save, the attacker is temporarily immune until the end of its next turn. The dazzling effect has the [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) traits.

 When you reach 17th level, the resistance increases to 10.
name: Starlight Armor

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs=""
>
[Touch Focus](/Feats.aspx?ID=4079) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Metamagic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=107" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Fists of the Ruby Phoenix Hardcover](/Sources.aspx?ID=193) pg. 223, [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 77

**Archetype** [Sixth Pillar](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=94)

**Prerequisites**
[Sixth Pillar Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2739)

**Trigger**
You begin to Cast a Spell that requires a spell attack roll.

</column>

---

You can redirect a spell's power through you to deliver it within striking distance. If your next action is to Cast a Spell with 1 or more targets and a range farther than touch, you change the range to touch. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the attack. This only allows you to touch a single target with the spell, even if the spell originally affected more than 1 target. As long as your spell attack is successful, the target is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) in addition to the spell's normal effects.
name: Touch Focus

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

[Tattoo Artist](/Feats.aspx?ID=2962); Master in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

</column>

---

Your craftsmanship at decorating flesh is unparalleled. You become legendary in Crafting. Whenever you craft a [magical tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?Category=46) whose level is at least 3 lower than your level, increase all attack roll and DC values of the tattoo's activated abilities by 1, and increase all resistances granted by the tattoo by 1.
name: Legendary Tattoo Artist

    right="Feat 7"
    pfs=""
>
[Quick Setup](/Feats.aspx?ID=4085) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 159

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

</column>

---

_Note from Nethys:_ this skill feat is intended to be used with the [Complex Crafting](/Rules.aspx?ID=1916) variant crafting rules.

If you reduce the setup time for a batch of [consumables](/Rules.aspx?ID=2951) at least 6 levels lower than you to less than 1 day, you can set it up in just 2 hours (allowing you to do so during an adventuring day if you have access to your tools and materials). If you rush the setup of a permanent item at least 6 levels lower than you and reduce the setup time to less than 1 day, you can perform the setup in 4 hours.
name: Quick Setup

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs=""
>
[Ursine Avenger Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=4086) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 183

**Archetype** [Ursine Avenger Hood](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=200)

</column>

---

You draw the _[Ursine Avenger Hood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2368)_ over your head and its fur over your arms, assuming an ursine form that has traits of your original form as well as that of a bear. In your ursine form, you gain a jaws [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack that deals 1d8 piercing damage and a claws unarmed attack that deals 1d6 slashing damage and has the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) trait. Both unarmed attacks are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group. You lose the ability to speak complex sentences while transformed and can only communicate through grunts and gestures; this prevents you from using effects that require a shared or spoken language until you revert back to your non-hybrid form. You can use this action while transformed to remove the hood and return to your original form.

<title level="2">Ursine Avenger Form leads to...</title>
[Fearsome Fangs](/Feats.aspx?ID=4093), [Great Bear](/Feats.aspx?ID=4091), [Immortal Bear](/Feats.aspx?ID=4095), [Terrible Transformation](/Feats.aspx?ID=4092)
name: Ursine Avenger Form

---

You can cast a 3rd-rank [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1694) as an [innate](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) spell, but only to summon a [black bear](/Monsters.aspx?ID=566). At 10th level, the _summon animal_ spell is heightened to 4th rank, and you can summon a [grizzly bear](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2850). At 12th level, your _summon animal_ innate spell is heightened to 5th rank, and you can summon a [polar bear](/Monsters.aspx?ID=567). At 14th level, it’s heightened to 6th rank, and you can summon a [cave bear](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2851).
name: Call Ursine Ally

Whether in the heart of a volcanic passageway or the glacial tundras of the Crown of the World, the only temperature you ever personally experience is an oddly comfortable chill. You are now protected from [extreme cold and extreme heat](/Rules.aspx?ID=642), and you gain resistance to fire equal to half your level. If you would already have resistance to fire equal to half your level from a heritage, ancestry feat, class feat, or another archetype feat, you instead gain resistance to fire equal to your level.

**Source** [Pathfinder #188: They Watched the Stars](/Sources.aspx?ID=194) pg. 82

**Archetype** [Scions of Domora](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=202)

**Prerequisites**
[Familiar Master Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1970); you have befriended a spirit guide and it bonded with you using its Bond with Mortal ability.

</column>

---

You gain a spirit guide ally. Your familiar takes on the form of a [spirit guide](/Familiars.aspx?Specific=true&ID=23) specific familiar.

**Special** You can take Scion of Domora Dedication even if you have the Familiar Master Dedication and have not taken two other [Familiar Master](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=60) feats. You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained at least two other feats from the scion of Domora or familiar master archetypes.

<title level="2">Scion of Domora Dedication leads to...</title>
[Guardian's Embrace](/Feats.aspx?ID=4109), [Guided Skill](/Feats.aspx?ID=4108), [Invigorating Breath](/Feats.aspx?ID=4110), [Spirit Guide Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=4113), [Spiritual Aid](/Feats.aspx?ID=4112), [Spiritual Flurry](/Feats.aspx?ID=4111), [Spiritual Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=4107)
name: Scion of Domora Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: [Familiar Master Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1970); you have befriended a spirit guide and it bonded with you using its Bond with Mortal ability.

    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs=""
>
[Spiritual Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=4107) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=73" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #188: They Watched the Stars](/Sources.aspx?ID=194) pg. 82

**Archetype** [Scions of Domora](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=202)

**Prerequisites**
[Scion of Domora Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4106)

**Requirements**
You are wielding a weapon with at least one [fundamental rune](/Rules.aspx?ID=738) and your familiar is adjacent to you or in your space.

</column>

---

Your spirit guide enhances your attacks with occult power. Make a Strike with a weapon that has at least one fundamental rune. This counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty. The Strike has the effects of the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune and deals an additional 2d6 force damage. If your weapon has a [_major striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) rune, this Strike instead deals 3d6 force damage.

<title level="2">Spiritual Strike leads to...</title>
[Spiritual Flurry](/Feats.aspx?ID=4111)
name: Spiritual Strike
requirement_markdown: You are wielding a weapon with at least one [fundamental rune](/Rules.aspx?ID=738) and your familiar is adjacent to you or in your space.

[Guardian's Embrace](/Feats.aspx?ID=4109) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

[Scion of Domora Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4106)

**Requirements**
You are adjacent to your spirit guide or it is sharing your space.

</column>

---

Your spirit guide discorporates to surround you in an invisible protective shroud. You gain a +2 status bonus to AC against physical attacks until the beginning of your next turn. If you are hit by a physical attack during this time, you can use a reaction to gain resistance 10 to physical damage dealt by that attack; doing so ends the AC bonus, and you can't use Guardian's Embrace again for 10 minutes. Your spirit guide cannot be targeted by effects, cannot be affected by area effects, and cannot take any actions while this ability is active. Your spirit guide appears in a space adjacent to you when the effect ends.
name: Guardian's Embrace
requirement_markdown: You are adjacent to your spirit guide or it is sharing your space.

markdown:
**Source** [Pathfinder #188: They Watched the Stars](/Sources.aspx?ID=194) pg. 83 **Archetype** [Scions of Domora](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=202) **Frequency** once per day **Prerequisites** [Scion of Domora Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4106) --- You fuse with your spirit guide, becoming a hybrid being with enhanced physical and spiritual power. This effect lasts 1 minute; you can end it early as a free action. During this time, you can't use any other actions granted by this archetype. You gain the following statistics and abilities, regardless of your type of spirit guide.
  • You become [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222).
  • You gain resistance 10 to all damage (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)).
  • [Darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415).
  • An [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) melee attack of the same type as your spirit guide (either claws or jaws), which is the only attack you can Strike with. You're trained with this attack. Your attack modifier is +29 or your normal unarmed attack modifier, whichever is higher. You use the listed statistics for the unarmed attack type:
  • **Claws** ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=172), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)) , **Damage** 3d6+5 slashing plus 1d6 force;
  • **Jaws** ([forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180)) , **Damage** 3d10+5 piercing plus 1d6 force.
name: Spirit Guide Form

    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Twilight Speaker Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4114) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #189: Dreamers of the Nameless Spires](/Sources.aspx?ID=195) pg. 78

**Archetype** [Twilight Speaker](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=203)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14); you are an Ilverani elf

</column>

---

You have carefully studied the histories, customs, and etiquette of many of the younger peoples of Golarion. You become an expert in Society. At 7th level, you become a master in Society, and at 15th level, you become legendary in Society. You can use the Society skill instead of the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) skill when you attempt to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on a non-[elven](/Traits.aspx?ID=58) intelligent [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) creature.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you've gained two other feats from the twilight speaker archetype.

<title level="2">Twilight Speaker Dedication leads to...</title>
[Betraying Shank](/Feats.aspx?ID=4117), [Disarming Smile](/Feats.aspx?ID=4116), [Empathetic Envoy](/Feats.aspx?ID=4115), [Ilverani Purist](/Feats.aspx?ID=4118), [World-Wise Vigilance](/Feats.aspx?ID=4119)
name: Twilight Speaker Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14); you are an Ilverani elf

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Empathetic Envoy](/Feats.aspx?ID=4115) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #189: Dreamers of the Nameless Spires](/Sources.aspx?ID=195) pg. 78

**Archetype** [Twilight Speaker](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=203)

**Prerequisites**
[Twilight Speaker Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4114)

</column>

---

You believe that treating others with respect is the fastest way into their hearts, and in turn others are more likely to believe in your good intentions and write off bad first impressions as flukes. If a creature's attitude towards you becomes lower over the course of a social interaction (for example, from [friendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=18) to [indifferent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=25), or from indifferent to [unfriendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=40)), their impression of you returns to its starting level an hour after the social interaction ends. This ability has no effect if the creature you are interacting with becomes [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=23).
name: Empathetic Envoy

markdown:
**Source** [Pathfinder #189: Dreamers of the Nameless Spires](/Sources.aspx?ID=195) pg. 78 **Archetype** [Twilight Speaker](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=203) **Prerequisites** [Twilight Speaker Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4114) **Trigger** You are the target of a melee attack and the attacker has not rolled yet. **Requirements** You are aware of the attacker, the attacker is an intelligent [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) creature, and you have not attempted to harm the attacker. --- With a wide, sincere smile, you give your attacker pause. You attempt a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) check against the triggering attacker's Will DC. After you use Disarming Smile, all creatures who witnessed you use it are temporarily immune to your Disarming Smile for the next 24 hours. **Critical Success** Your enemy ceases their attack. The attack fails and the triggering target can't attempt [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against you until the beginning of its next turn or until you (or your allies) take hostile actions against the enemy (or its allies). You can begin talking to the creature on your next turn to attempt another Diplomacy check; on a success, you sustain the effect until the beginning of your next turn, to a maximum of 1 minute. Talking on subsequent rounds requires that you be able to communicate with the target creature and imparts the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) and [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) traits to the action.
**Success** Your foe pauses momentarily. Their attack fails, but they can attempt further attacks against you.
**Failure** The target's attack is unaffected. Disarming Smile leads to... [Emphatic Emissary](/Feats.aspx?ID=4120)
name: Disarming Smile
requirement_markdown: You are aware of the attacker, the attacker is an intelligent [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) creature, and you have not attempted to harm the attacker.

    right="Feat 6"

>
[Betraying Shank](/Feats.aspx?ID=4117) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

While your usual diplomacy is well intentioned, you know that sometimes those same skills will need to be turned to deception. In a flash, you draw a sheathed or concealed [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) or [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179) weapon and make a melee Strike. The target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against your Strike. You then roll [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) for initiative.
name: Betraying Shank

>
[Ilverani Purist](/Feats.aspx?ID=4118) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />

[Twilight Speaker Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4114)

</column>

---

All twilight speakers are trained to resist the alluring customs of younger peoples, but you have made it your mandate to keep the Ilverani way unchanged. Such dedication has given you a trained eye for subtlety and deception. When you attempt to [Sense the Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85) of a non-[elf](/Traits.aspx?ID=58) [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) creature and you roll a critical failure, you fail instead.
name: Ilverani Purist

</column>

---

You can tell when diplomacy has failed and a meeting is close to unraveling or falling to blows. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus on your initiative roll. During your first turn in combat, you can use your Disarming Smile, targeting every [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=23) intelligent [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) creature that can see you but has yet to act. If you choose to sustain your Disarming Smile, you sustain the effect for only one creature, as normal.

---

You can track down targets with the help of locals. You can use Diplomacy or Society, whichever you're trained in, to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) creatures in settlements. You chat with locals to help follow the trail of creatures you Track. At the GM's discretion, there might not be enough people to speak with to keep following the trail.

[Fleeing Diversion](/Feats.aspx?ID=4122) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 76

Expert in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5)

</column>

---

You are quick to capitalize on your distractions. If you critically succeed at your check to [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=2387) against the majority of the creatures (at least half of the creatures) whose attention you're trying to divert, you can use your reaction to immediately [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404), [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405), or [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304). As normal, you are only [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) to creatures against which you succeed on your check to Create a Diversion, even if you manage to Hide or Sneak away.
name: Fleeing Diversion

[Half-Truths](/Feats.aspx?ID=4123) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 76

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5); Expert in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6)

</column>

---

You are able to weave your words in such a way that you never use actual lies, but still mislead people to certain conclusions. You can attempt to make a [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) of a creature using Deception instead of Diplomacy. You can attempt to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) to a creature using [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) instead of Deception. If you fail your check to Lie, the creature realizes that you were twisting words to fool them without outright lying.
name: Half-Truths

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rolling Landing](/Feats.aspx?ID=4124) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 76

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Rolling Landing’s text only affects the reaction used after a fall, not any reaction._

**Prerequisites**
[Cat Fall](/Feats.aspx?ID=765); Trained in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1)

</column>

---

You land with quick rolls that help you keep your momentum. If you [fall](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) more than 5 feet and don't take damage (usually due to treating the fall as a shorter distance), you can use your reaction to immediately enter a short roll when you land and Step. If you are an expert in Acrobatics, you can use your reaction to Step or Stride up to half your Speed. If you're a master in Acrobatics, you can use your reaction to Step or Stride up to your full Speed. If you're legendary in Acrobatics, you don't trigger reactions triggered by movement when you Stride in this way.
name: Rolling Landing

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Slippery Prey](/Feats.aspx?ID=4125) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 76

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) or Trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

---

You're able to escape bonds more easily than others. When you attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) using Acrobatics or Athletics, you reduce the multiple attack penalty for repeated attempts to –4 and –8 if you're trained in the skill. The penalty becomes –3 and –6 if you're a master in the appropriate skill. If you're legendary in the skill, you don't take penalties for multiple Escape attempts in the same turn. Regardless of your training, your attempts to Escape still have the [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15) trait and incur a multiple attack penalty with other actions.
name: Slippery Prey

    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Devrin's Dazzling Diversion](/Feats.aspx?ID=4126) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Rogue" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=139" />
<trait label="Swashbuckler" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=321" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 76

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5)

**Access**
Members of the Firebrands at the rank of second mark or higher have access to this feat.

</column>

---

Devrin Arlos has a habit of using whatever is at hand to temporarily blind others in combat. Copying his technique, you make use of your cloak, the light reflecting from your weapon, a handful of flower petals, or some other physical distraction as part of your feint, leaving your opponent unable to see for a moment. When you successfully [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48) a creature, it becomes [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the end of your turn. If you critically succeed, the creature is dazzled until the start of your next turn. The creature can use an Interaction to remove the dazzled condition. As normal, you can dazzle only creatures that use vision or a similar sense, as determined by the GM.
name: Devrin's Dazzling Diversion

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 76 **Prerequisites** Trained in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) **Access** Members of the Firebrands at the rank of second mark or higher have access to this feat. **Requirements** You're wielding two one-handed firearms. --- Every good Firebrand knows that style can be as important as substance—so long as you don't screw up the delivery. Make a Performance check against an [easy](/Rules.aspx?ID=555) [DC for your level](/Rules.aspx?ID=554) as you spin your guns around and juggle ammunition to reload them. **Critical Success** You Interact to reload up to two firearms you are currently wielding.
**Success** You Interact to reload a single firearm you are currently wielding.
**Failure** You drop one of the required firearms and a round of ammunition on the ground in your space.
**Critical Failure** You drop both firearms and two rounds on the ground in your space.
name: Ostentatious Reload

    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Nightwave Springing Reload](/Feats.aspx?ID=4128) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Gunslinger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=406" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 77

**Access**
Members of the Firebrands at the rank of second mark or higher have access to this feat.

</column>

---

Training with the crew of the Nightwave has taught you their technique for reloading while leaping to board another ship. You [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81) and then Interact to reload, or Interact to reload and then Leap. You can perform this ability as a 2-action activity to use your Leap to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37) without needing to Stride at least 10 feet first.
name: Nightwave Springing Reload

    level="1"
    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tumbling Theft](/Feats.aspx?ID=4129) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 77

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1); Expert in [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17)

</column>

---

You reach for an opponent's item as you move past a foe. If you critically succeed your check to [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=29) an enemy's space, you can attempt to [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) something from the enemy as a reaction. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your Thievery check to Steal as your tumbling makes it difficult for your enemy to keep track of your movement. You can Steal any immediately accessible item of light or lighter Bulk on the enemy's person, such as a potion or coin purse hanging from a belt, but not anything inside a container or anything the enemy is holding. The GM has final say on what you can Steal.
name: Tumbling Theft

    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Helt's Spelldance](/Feats.aspx?ID=4130) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Bard" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=19" />
<trait label="Sorcerer" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=148" />
<trait label="Witch" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=323" />
<trait label="Wizard" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=166" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 77

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12)

**Access**
Members of the Firebrands at the rank of second mark or higher have access to this feat.

</column>

---

Ranik Helt knows a special technique to hide his spells as part of a performance, and you've learned this technique. Ostentatiously performing, you move across the battlefield, taking foes aback and creating an opening for your spell. Attempt a Performance check against all observers' Perception DCs. Then, Stride once or twice. During this movement, you don't trigger reactions that are triggered by your movement from the creatures against which you succeeded on your Performance check. After your movement, you can Cast a Spell. If you used one Stride, you can cast either a 1-action or a 2-action spell; if you used two Strides, you can only cast a 1-action spell. If you critically succeed on your check against any creature, they take a –1 circumstance penalty to their saving throws against any spell you cast as part of your Helt's Spelldance due to your distracting performance. All creatures that observed you become temporarily immune to your Helt's Spelldance for 10 minutes.
name: Helt's Spelldance

You perform a bewildering show of prowess, such as by whirling and flashing a weapon, that unnerves foes. Attempt Intimidation checks to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) each enemy within 30 feet. If your last action was a critical hit against an enemy or reduced an enemy to 0 Hit Points, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your Intimidation checks. Regardless of the results of your checks, each creature is then temporarily immune to Dazzling Display for 1 minute.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 78

**Archetypes** [Alter Ego](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=187), [Archaeologist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=46) (Level 3)

**Prerequisites**
[Alter Ego Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3807) or [Archaeologist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1879); Trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)

**Access**
Members of the Firebrands at the rank of second mark or higher have access to this feat.

**Trigger**
An ally you can see and hear is about to attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) someone or [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47).

</column>

---

Emulating Shensen whenever she arrives at a show, you share your knowledge of the region and drop a few important local names to make a good impression and help your allies do the same. Attempt a [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) check to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=75) your ally. If you succeed, you recall a few specific details about local culture and personalities to help support your ally's deception. In addition to the benefits of your Aid, the triggering ally rolls their Deception check twice and takes the better result. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.
name: We're on the List
prerequisite_markdown: [Alter Ego Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3807) or [Archaeologist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1879); Trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 78 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The Critical Success effect of Devrin’s Cunning Stance to deny an enemy’s reaction only applies if an ally’s Strike is successful against a flat-footed enemy._ **Archetype** [Marshal](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=254) **Prerequisites** [Marshal Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2005); Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) **Access** Members of the Firebrands at the rank of second mark or higher have access to this feat. --- You've learned Devrin Arlos's guileful ways, and his sly teachings help you remind your allies to keep their wits about them. When you use this action, attempt a Deception check. The DC is usually a [standard-difficulty DC of your level](/Rules.aspx?ID=554), but the GM can assign a different DC based on the circumstances. The effect depends on the result of your check. **Critical Success** Your marshal's aura increases to a 20-foot emanation, and it grants you and your allies a +1 status bonus to skill checks. When you or an ally in the aura successfully Strike a [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) enemy, that enemy can't use reactions until the beginning of its next turn.
**Success** As critical success, but your aura's size doesn't increase.
**Failure** You fail to enter the stance.
**Critical Failure** You fail to enter the stance and can't take this action again for 1 minute.
name: Devrin's Cunning Stance

Rebellions require an intricate knowledge of one's surroundings—because you might need to escape at any moment. You remember the locations of escape routes, ambush spots, and cover from a scouting expedition or a previous visit to the area. Roll a d20. At any point during the subsequent combat, you can substitute the result of this roll instead of rolling a d20 on one [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check to [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=45) or on one [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62).

</column>

---

The Sapphire Butterfly is not one for violence when simply intimidating a foe will do. From that vigilante's techniques, you've learned how to pull a deadly blow at the last moment, threatening your target instead of striking them. Rather than dealing damage with the triggering Strike, attempt an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check with a +2 circumstance bonus to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) all enemies within 30 feet who can see you. If you critically succeed at your check to Demoralize the target of your Strike, their attitude toward you improves by one step while they also become [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
name: Tut-Tut

<traits>

Some Firebrands can't stand it when their allies are ruining their moment, and you're no different. Your loud and forceful condemnation of an ally succumbing to an incapacitation effect gives them a second chance to impress you and shake off the effect. Your ally rerolls the triggering Will saving throw with a +1 circumstance bonus. Regardless of the result of the save, your ally is temporarily immune to You're an Embarrassment! for 10 minutes.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Watch This!](/Feats.aspx?ID=4140) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 79

**Archetypes** [Duelist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=248), [Pirate](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=70) (Level 6)

**Prerequisites**
[Duelist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1956) or [Pirate Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2032)

**Access**
Members of the Firebrands at the rank of second mark or higher have access to this feat.

**Trigger**
An ally misses or critically misses an enemy you can see with their attack.

</column>

---

A common saying among Firebrands is, “There's always a fool who thinks they can do it better—and they're often right.” You verbally commit to using an action on your next turn to make a melee Strike against the same enemy your ally has missed. If your attack hits, you deal additional damage equal to twice the number of damage dice.
name: Watch This!

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Living for the Applause](/Feats.aspx?ID=4142) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 80

**Archetype** [Gladiator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=251)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Gladiator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1976)

**Access**
Members of the Firebrands at the rank of second mark or higher have access to this feat.

**Trigger**
You would be reduced to 0 Hit Points (but not immediately killed) during a combat encounter that has spectators.

</column>

---

Nothing can stop you as long as you have an audience cheering you on. The attention reinvigorates you and keeps you from being knocked out. You remain at 1 Hit Point, and your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) condition increases by 1.

 In addition, attempt a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) check; the DC is determined by the GM but is typically the [standard DC for your level](/Rules.aspx?ID=554) or the DC to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on the spectators, whichever is higher. On a success, the crowd erupts in applause and cheers at your comeback, much to the dismay of your enemies. Concern for the crowd's reaction should your opponent deliver a finishing blow gives you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the end of your next turn. On a critical success, you don't expend your daily use of Living for the Applause and can use the reaction an additional time on the same day.
name: Living for the Applause

    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Public Execution](/Feats.aspx?ID=4143) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=73" />
<trait label="Linguistic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=101" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 80

**Archetype** [Assassin](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=239)

**Prerequisites**
[Assassin Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1890)

**Access**
Members of the Firebrands at the rank of second mark or higher have access to this feat.

**Requirements**
You have designated a mark using Mark for Death.

</column>

---

 Some rebel Firebrand cells make a practice of publicly declaring the main target of a given operation. You announce your intention to kill your mark for all to hear and reveal the reason they must die, perhaps listing their crimes or sharing a damning secret. Creatures within 30 feet who can see and hear you are shocked by your proclamation or otherwise reluctant to come to your mark's aid. They must attempt a Will save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. On a failure, the creature can't attack you or otherwise perform [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against you until the end of your next turn. If you use a hostile action against a creature other than your mark, this effect ends. Your mark becomes temporarily immune to your Public Execution for 1 day.
name: Public Execution
requirement_markdown: You have designated a mark using Mark for Death.

    pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=73" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 80

**Archetype** [Acrobat](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=236)

**Frequency**
once per minute

**Prerequisites**
[Acrobat Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1873)

**Access**
Members of the Firebrands at the rank of second mark or higher have access to this feat.

**Trigger**
You succeed at an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) or [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check deemed suitably challenging and meaningful by the GM.

</column>

---

As is the tradition with many Firebrands, you call your shot and look to impress with an extra bit of panache or a greater stunt. Attempt the triggering check again, using the second result. On a success, you gain the normal results of the check, plus you feel invigorated by your stunt and gain temporary Hit Points equal to your level (double your level on a critical success). You lose any remaining temporary Hit Points after 1 minute. Typically, a challenging task is one with at least a [standard DC for your level](/Rules.aspx?ID=554), though the difficulty may be higher depending on the situation. A meaningful context is one where the action's success or failure is relevant to the pursuit of your goals.
name: Show-Off

    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Big Debut](/Feats.aspx?ID=4146) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 80

**Archetypes** [Celebrity](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=245), [Gladiator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=61) (Level 10), [Gladiator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=251) (Level 10)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Celebrity Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1931); [Gladiator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1976)

**Access**
Members of the Firebrands at the rank of second mark or higher have access to this feat.

**Trigger**
You're about to roll initiative.

</column>

---

Now's your chance! You're a Firebrand, and the world must know it. You strike a dramatic pose, deliver a snappy quip, or reveal a shocking new costume, captivating everyone who can see you. You don't roll initiative. Instead, you voluntarily go last. Enemies who can see you must attempt a Will saving throw against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. On a failure, the enemy is overcome with anticipation for what you'll do next on the battlefield and becomes [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) (stunned 2 on a critical failure). As your first action on your first turn, you can Interact to draw or stow an item of light Bulk as a free action; if this item is a small garment or accessory, such as a fan, a pair of gloves, or a wine glass, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus on Charisma-based skill checks until the end of your turn.
name: Big Debut

>
[Hook 'Em](/Feats.aspx?ID=4147) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=73" />
</traits>
